formations_blocks: Switzerland in sustained early possession, mostly in their own half
Canada defending compactly enough to force Switzerland into harmless possession
key_matchups: Rodriguez vs Embolo on the through ball into the box
Crepeau vs Embolo at the shot
Manzambi vs Cornelius on the rebound
Ali Ahmed's through pass finding Larin
Kobel vs Larin in the one-on-one
Buchanan's follow-up blocked by a defender
phases.summary: Switzerland controlled the ball early but much of their possession was cautious and deep, with Canada content to sit off and look for transitions. The first real chance came when Rodriguez found Embolo, but Crepeau saved well and Cornelius blocked the follow-up, before Canada briefly threatened on the break through Larin, only for the move to be ruled offside.

formations_blocks: passive early phase with both teams not pressing intensely
Canada starting to come into the game
adjustments: Buchanan moving into attacking moments from the right on set-piece and open play
Larin pulling from centre to the left to receive and attack inside
key_matchups: Xhaka vs Larin in the free-kick incident
phases.summary: The phase begins with Canada passive but gradually growing into the contest, though the overall tempo and pressing remain modest. Canada’s best moments come from direct attacks: a right-sided free-kick that leads to a shot, and a break where Larin drifts left, cuts inside and forces a save. Switzerland also threaten in transition, with quick passing releasing Vargas on the left, but neither side establishes sustained control.

formations_blocks: considering a switch to five at the back at half-time
adjustments: Jesse Marsch considered switching to a five-at-the-back to lock things down
Alphonso Davies was considered as a possible half-time change but not used due to load management
phases.summary: The first half is described as tentative, with Canada not sprinting enough and the match still 0-0. The only concrete tactical note is Marsch’s admission that he considered moving to a five-at-the-back at half-time to make the team more secure, while also weighing but ultimately postponing an Alphonso Davies introduction.

adjustments: Switzerland worked efficiently down the right to create the opening goal
Canada had been quiet in attack and the match state appeared to dull their threat
Canada sent a good cross from the left, but their central attacking target was not connected with
Switzerland then punished Canada with a long angled pass behind the defence and a quick supporting run into the box
key_matchups: Embalo vs Canada's defender on the first ball into the box
phases.summary: Switzerland began the second half with a sharp right-sided attack, opening the scoring through Vargas after Manzambi’s cross evaded Embolo. Canada struggled to build momentum or threaten consistently, and Switzerland doubled their lead when a direct ball behind the defence and a simple square pass freed Manzambi for a finish. The phase was defined by Switzerland’s efficient attacking transitions and Canada’s lack of attacking jeopardy.

formations_blocks: Switzerland defending under sustained late pressure
adjustments: Switzerland: Dan Ndoye on for Ruben Vargas
Canada: Jacob Shaffelburg on for Richie Laryea
Switzerland: Christian Fassnacht and Cedric Itten on for Breel Embolo and Manzambi
key_matchups: Saliba vs Canada’s defensive line/keeper on the build-up to the goal
Promise David vs Kobel on the 76' finish
Johnston vs Kobel on late headers/crosses
Cornelius vs Switzerland’s box defending on set pieces
phases.summary: Switzerland struck first in the phase through a direct inside-right pass and a sharp finish from Promise David, then spent much of the remainder under pressure as Canada pushed for an equaliser. Canada created a few late chances from crosses, free-kicks and long throws, but Switzerland survived the set-piece barrage and also threatened on the break after a corner, with Kobel making key late interventions.
